payload.outputSummary:
19:          approval_policy: String.t() | map(),
57:           approval_policy: session_policies.approval_policy,
58:           auto_approve_requests: session_policies.approval_policy == "never",
79:          approval_policy: approval_policy,
97:    case start_turn(port, thread_id, prompt, issue, workspace, approval_policy, turn_sandbox_policy) do
347:         %{approval_policy: approval_policy, thread_sandbox: thread_sandbox},
354:        "approvalPolicy" => approval_policy,
373:  defp start_turn(port, thread_id, prompt, issue, workspace, approval_policy, turn_sandbox_policy) do
387:        "approvalPolicy" => approval_policy,
      {:error, reason} -> {:error, reason}
    end
  end

  defp start_thread(
         port,
         workspace,
         %{approval_policy: approval_policy, thread_sandbox: thread_sandbox},
         dynamic_tool_binding
       ) do
    send_message(port, %{
      "method" => "thread/start",
      "id" => @thread_start_id,
      "params" => %{
        "approvalPolicy" => approval_policy,
        "sandbox" => thread_sandbox,
        "cwd" => workspace,
        "dynamicTools" => dynamic_tool_binding.tool_specs
      }
    })

    case await_response(port, @thread_start_id) do
      {:ok, %{"thread" => thread_payload}} ->
        case thread_payload do
          %{"id" => thread_id} -> {:ok, thread_id}
          _ -> {:error, {:invalid_thread_payload, thread_payload}}
        end

      other ->
        other
    end
  end

  defp start_turn(port, thread_id, prompt, issue, workspace, approval_policy, turn_sandbox_policy) do
    send_message(port, %{
      "method" => "turn/start",
      "id" => @turn_start_id,
      "params" => %{
        "threadId" => thread_id,
        "input" => [
          %{
            "type" => "text",
            "text" => prompt
          }
        ],
        "cwd" => workspace,
        "title" => "#{issue.identifier}: #{issue.title}",
        "approvalPolicy" => approval_policy,
        "sandboxPolicy" => turn_sandbo

19:          approval_policy: String.t() | map(),
57:           approval_policy: session_policies.approval_policy,
58:           auto_approve_requests: session_policies.approval_policy == "never",
79:          approval_policy: approval_policy,
97:    case start_turn(port, thread_id, prompt, issue, workspace, approval_policy, turn_sandbox_policy) do
347:         %{approval_policy: approval_policy, thread_sandbox: thread_sandbox},
354:        "approvalPolicy" => approval_policy,
373:  defp start_turn(port, thread_id, prompt, issue, workspace, approval_policy, turn_sandbox_policy) do
387:        "approvalPolicy" => approval_policy,
      {:error, reason} -> {:error, reason}
    end
  end

  defp start_thread(
         port,
         workspace,
         %{approval_policy: approval_policy, thread_sandbox: thread_sandbox},
         dynamic_tool_binding
       ) do
    send_message(port, %{
      "method" => "thread/start",
      "id" => @thread_start_id,
      "params" => %{
        "approvalPolicy" => approval_policy,
        "sandbox" => thread_sandbox,
        "cwd" => workspace,
        "dynamicTools" => dynamic_tool_binding.tool_specs
      }
    })

    case await_response(port, @thread_start_id) do
      {:ok, %{"thread" => thread_payload}} ->
        case thread_payload do
          %{"id" => thread_id} -> {:ok, thread_id}
          _ -> {:error, {:invalid_thread_payload, thread_payload}}
        end

      other ->
        other
    end
  end

  defp start_turn(port, thread_id, prompt, issue, workspace, approval_policy, turn_sandbox_policy) do
    send_message(port, %{
      "method" => "turn/start",
      "id" => @turn_start_id,
      "params" => %{
        "threadId" => thread_id,
        "input" => [
          %{
            "type" => "text",
            "text" => prompt
          }
        ],
        "cwd" => workspace,
        "title" => "#{issue.identifier}: #{issue.title}",
        "approvalPolicy" => approval_policy,
        "sandboxPolicy" => turn_sandbox_policy
      }
    })

    case await_response(port, @turn_start_id) do
      {:ok, %{"turn" => %{"id" => turn_id}}} -> {:ok, turn_id}
      other -> other
    end

changes.diff:
@@ -154,2 +154,3 @@
 - Supported `codex.approval_policy` values depend on the targeted Codex app-server version. In the current local Codex schema, string values include `untrusted`, `on-failure`, `on-request`, and `never`, and object-form `reject` is also supported.
+- BOS AgentRun turns always use `untrusted` command classification so Symphony can inspect commands before launch. Direct `gh` execution and detectable `api.github.com` REST/GraphQL access are denied and durably audited with the corresponding `bos-mcp` operation; ordinary Git transport remains available. This does not govern host-side workspace hooks or human administration.
 - Supported `codex.thread_sandbox` values: `read-only`, `workspace-write`, `danger-full-access`.

@@ -96,3 +96,5 @@
 
-    case start_turn(port, thread_id, prompt, issue, workspace, approval_policy, turn_sandbox_policy) do
+    turn_approval_policy = if bos_agent_run?(issue), do: "untrusted", else: approval_policy
+
+    case start_turn(port, thread_id, prompt, issue, workspace, turn_approval_policy, turn_sandbox_policy) do
       {:ok, turn_id} ->

@@ -154,2 +154,3 @@

This does not govern host-side workspace hooks or human administration.

diff.summary:
diff --git a/elixir/README.md b/elixir/README.md
index 25f5411874899ee820b60ab47c087d00ece7e2b2..f6a0b27f358f38c2b7e310c6aa597f106f980930
--- a/elixir/README.md
+++ b/elixir/README.md
@@ -152,6 +152,7 @@
   - `codex.thread_sandbox` defaults to `workspace-write`
   - `codex.turn_sandbox_policy` defaults to a `workspaceWrite` policy rooted at the current issue workspace
 - Supported `codex.approval_policy` values depend on the targeted Codex app-server version. In the current local Codex schema, string values include `untrusted`, `on-failure`, `on-request`, and `never`, and object-form `reject` is also supported.
+- BOS AgentRun turns always use `untrusted` command classification so Symphony can inspect commands before launch. Direct `gh` execution and detectable `api.github.com` REST/GraphQL access are denied and durably audited with the corresponding `bos-mcp` operation; ordinary Git transport remains available. This does not govern host-side workspace hooks or human administration.
 - Supported `codex.thread_sandbox` values: `read-only`, `workspace-write`, `danger-full-access`.
 - When `codex.turn_sandbox_policy` is set explicitly, Symphony passes the map through to Codex
   unchanged. Compatibility then depends on the targeted Codex app-server version rather than local

         |> auditable_item?()

+        true
+
       true ->
         false

+  defp event_type("bos/commandPolicy/blocked", _payload), do: "command.policy_blocked"

   defp item_event_type(payload, suffix) do
@@ -574,6 +579,7 @@

   defp event_status(%{event: :turn_cancelled}), do: "cancelled"
+  defp event_status(%{event: :command_policy_blocked}), do: "blocked"
   defp event_status(_update), do: "completed"
 
   defp summary(method, %{"command" => command} = payload)
